allocations.page_job: Tell Lena within five seconds that this is an affirming, intellectually honest, non-denominational church in Redlands that will not ask her family to perform belief — then route each persona to their on-ramp.

section_intents.section_job: Lena and Jordan scan for one disqualifying signal; the hero disarms them with the x-factor in plain language while carrying the affirming + non-denominational + Redlands SEO phrases.
A visitor knows what they're being invited to — an hour thinking about something that matters, Saturdays 10:00 AM at 51 W Olive — before they scroll further.
The deconstructor, the LGBTQ+ family, the mixed household, and the questioner each see themselves named as the reason Paradox is built this way — architecture, not asterisk.
Marcus can sample the teaching right now: sermons start discussions, the current Matthew series is live, and the weekly blog continues it.
Every persona sees a legible next move at their stage: visit, join a discussion group, check the kids' pages, grab coffee with the pastor — a pathway, never a funnel.
A visitor learns Paradox exists for a Redlands where more people are becoming more loving — with Hunger Walk, Food Drive, and Shoe Drive as proof, not aspiration.
Lena leaves with one specific, low-friction action: plan a visit for a Saturday — and she can tell us she's coming.
The page closes from alongside: a 2016 origin note, 'we're still figuring this out, and we'd love to figure it out with you.'

allocations.page_job: Answer every unasked first-timer question — what the service is actually like, what time, where to park, what happens with the kids, and 'is this really a safe place for my family?' — before anyone has to ask.

section_intents.section_job: A first-time family feels received, not recruited: 'we are so glad you found us' with zero performance pressure.
In 90 seconds a visitor knows the shape of a Saturday: 75 minutes, modern music, thought-provoking sermon, hospitality team, reflective moments.

The skeptical partner and the worried parent both exhale: you can disagree with the pastor, music is invitational, questions are part of the journey, and the affirming reality is stated without disclaimer.
Parents know exactly what happens with their kids: ParaTots / Kids / Youth at 9:15, what's taught, who teaches, and the Parents Room escape valve.
Every remaining unasked question gets a plain answer: parking, why Saturdays, the name, communion, denomination.
A real voice says what it felt like to land here, and coffee with the pastor is offered as the human-sized next step.
The family commits: 'let us know you're coming' — one form, kids pre-noted, questions welcome.
'We'll be looking for you' — a warm, specific send-off with the address and time once more.

allocations.page_job: Give Marcus long-form, intellectually serious teaching he can sample immediately — and make the 89-sermon archive browsable, filterable, and SEO-strong.
allocations.primary_persona: Marcus, the Skeptic Who Came Back Twice

section_intents.section_job: Marcus reads the teaching posture in one line: sermons start discussions rather than end them, and disagreement is welcome.
The visitor grasps the spine: a nine-year journey through every book of the Bible, completed once, begun again — currently the Gospel of Matthew.
The archive itself: 89 sermons with series taxonomy, discussion guides, notes, audio — plus watch-live and podcast for the online audience.

sources.note: cms_managed: sermons CPT per tech flags; preserve URL equity with redirects.
Applies site-wide; outline-page should mark matching sections cms_managed.

section_intents.section_job: The sermon doesn't end at the door: keep the discussion going in a midweek group.
One action: watch or listen to the latest message right now.
Close on the posture: you don't have to agree with the pastor to belong here.

allocations.page_job: Let Jordan and Marcus read the whole story end-to-end — origin, name, mission, vision, milestones, testimonies, leadership — and find the welcome structurally consistent at every layer.
allocations.primary_persona: Jordan, the Exile Coming Home Slowly

section_intents.section_job: Open with the 2016 origin moment — friends in a living room building a church with arms, minds, and hearts wide open — not a generic welcome.
The reader places Paradox: non-denominational with Adventist roots, ~100 people, Saturday gatherings, downtown Redlands.
Mission and vision in their exact words: see and embrace Jesus Christ in all — all 168 hours — toward a Redlands where more people are becoming more loving.
Ten years of receipts: a milestone timeline from the living room to the 10-Year Celebration — including the 2018 affirming commitment, women's empowerment, and anti-racism.
People who stayed say why, in their own faces and voices: the testimony library proves the welcome is real.

sources.note: 9 testimonials exceed testimonial_video max_items 1 — render as cms_managed carousel/grid, not static slots.

section_intents.section_job: A snapshot of who carries the church — lead pastor, staff, and an elder board that is half female — with the full directory one click away.
Jordan takes a low-stakes step: read the beliefs, or get coffee with the pastor.
Close from alongside: we are still figuring this out, and we would love to figure it out with you.

allocations.page_job: Give Marcus and Lena the theological honesty they need before trusting anything else: what Paradox actually believes, what it refuses to claim, and the five values — in carefully tended language.
allocations.primary_persona: Marcus, the Skeptic Who Came Back Twice

section_intents.section_job: One line of distinctive POV: you don't have to choose between your intellect and your faith — and nothing here is watered down.
The shape of the faith in one glance: One Core Belief, Two Commandments, Three Practices.
The beliefs breathe in Paradox's own voice: foundation on Jesus, paradox embraced rather than resolved, scholarship as a doorway to God.
The five named values, in the partner's exact words — the standard, not the slogan.

sources.note: 5 values exceed feature_tabbed max_items 4 — outline-page should split 3+2 across paired sections or pick a 5-slot pattern; flagged so bind doesn't fail.

section_intents.section_job: Where Paradox stands, specifically: full LGBTQ+ affirmation, women's empowerment, anti-racism, weekly open-table communion, sexual ethics, denominational status.
The reader tests it in person or in conversation: ask the pastor anything over coffee.
Close with humility: nobody here has a monopoly on God. We mean that.

allocations.page_job: Give the affirming identity its own searchable home so 'affirming church Redlands' lands here and Jordan or Lena can verify — structurally, not decoratively — before ever visiting.
allocations.primary_persona: Jordan, the Exile Coming Home Slowly

section_intents.section_job: Open and affirming, on purpose: stated confidently and specifically, as architecture rather than asterisk.

sources.note: Passage lives under the giving crawl topic; reuse the claim here.

section_intents.section_job: The reader learns this wasn't an amendment: 2018 commitment, queer speakers since 2017, weddings since 2019 — built that way.
What structural inclusion looks like between Saturdays: Paradox after Dark, the sexuality statement, a blog that says the quiet parts out loud.
For the person searching 'affirming non-denominational church Redlands': the plain facts, in heading-visible language.
Jordan's low-stakes step: visit a Saturday, or verify over coffee first.
Close on celebration, not tolerance: you can bring your whole self, including the parts you were told to be ashamed of.

allocations.page_job: Show Marcus the real people — pastor, staff, elders, trustees — through a CMS-managed grid that also kills the hand-placed-image workflow pain.
allocations.primary_persona: Marcus, the Skeptic Who Came Back Twice

section_intents.section_job: Meet the people: friends who are figuring it out alongside you, not a hierarchy above you.
The staff, with faces, roles, pronouns, and direct emails — managed in WordPress, not hand-placed images.

sources.note: cms_managed: staff CPT per AM handoff; grid exceeds feature_team static slots by design.

section_intents.section_job: Governance with receipts: an Elder Board that is half female (including the Head Elder) and a Trustee Board that keeps the church accountable.

sources.note: cms_managed staff CPT; boards grouped by role taxonomy.

section_intents.section_job: Say hello to a real person — no funnel, just a reply.
Close warm and plain: these are the people who will be looking for you on Saturday.

allocations.page_job: Make the weekly sermon-based blog a real destination for Marcus's re-entry and the SEO compounding the audit calls for.
allocations.primary_persona: Marcus, the Skeptic Who Came Back Twice

section_intents.section_job: Reading is a legitimate way in: essays on ethics, politics, queer affirmation, women's empowerment, and racial justice from the Sunday-after angle.
The post archive — weekly, sermon-based, filterable — rendered from WordPress.

sources.note: cms_managed: blog post type; weekly sermon-based cadence per AM handoff.

section_intents.section_job: Each post is the sermon continued — same conversation, new medium — pointing back to the archive and forward to a group.
Subscribe once, get the week's thinking delivered.
Close with the standing offer: keep reading, keep disagreeing, keep the discussion going.

allocations.page_job: Turn Jordan's 'find my people' need into one concrete move: pick a group, starting with Pastor Craig's — honest conversation, not small-group curriculum.
allocations.primary_persona: Jordan, the Exile Coming Home Slowly

section_intents.section_job: Sermons start conversations, not end them — groups are where the conversation actually happens.
A newcomer knows exactly where to start: Pastor Craig's hybrid group is the recommended first room.
The groups themselves: Tuesdays, Wednesdays, every-other-Friday, and the drop-in after-service circle — with leaders named.

sources.note: cms_managed: groups type per content collection; wordpress_managed display.

section_intents.section_job: The discussion-guide library, finally in one place — this week's guide and the archive.

sources.note: cms_managed: discussion guides CPT per tech flags.

section_intents.section_job: What a group is actually like: processing, questioning, growing — no answers required to belong.
One move: join a group this week (or just show up after the service).
Close with the standing welcome: the seat in the circle is already there.

allocations.page_job: Reassure Lena that ages 5-9 get curiosity-first formation — what's taught, who teaches, and how kids actually lead — written for the skeptical-partner read.

section_intents.section_job: Signal joy and curiosity first: a space where kids explore faith through creativity and hands-on involvement.
Parents see what's actually taught and by whom: same Bible books as the main service, parent-teachers, age-appropriate framing — no conformity pressure.
The logistics in one glance: Saturdays 9:15, ages 5-9, Sermon Scavenger Hunt, then everyone together at 10:00.
Kids don't just attend — they lead: liturgists, communion helpers, singers — plus the events they talk about all year.
Join Saturday at 9:15 — and stick around for the main service at 10:00.
Close on what Lena actually wants: the kind of adults her kids become.

allocations.page_job: Tell parents of under-5s their littlest ones are safe, loved, and learning belonging from the very beginning.

section_intents.section_job: Lead with the line that melts a parent: before you were even born, God loved you first.
The room is safe and the parents aren't alone: stories, songs, play — and a Parents Room where you can breathe.
Logistics: ages 0-4, Saturdays 9:15, Donna Hadley ('Teacher Donna') coordinating.
Join us at 9:15, then stay for the 10:00 gathering together.
Close on solidarity: we are all in this mystery of parenting together.

allocations.page_job: Show parents of 10-14s a space that feels like their kid's own — honest talk, real participation, background-checked leaders.

section_intents.section_job: A space that feels like theirs: friends, snacks, projects, and honest talk about life and faith.
Parents get the safety and substance answers: background-checked leaders, kids treated as contributors, not observers.
Logistics and rhythm: Saturdays 9:15-9:50 for ages 10-14, then the Scavenger Hunt carries them into the 10:00 service.
Adults who love this age group make it work — and there's room for parents' gifts on the team.
Bring them Saturday at 9:15 — they'll tell you about it all week.
Close on belonging: a community that feels safe enough to ask anything.

allocations.page_job: Reach University of Redlands students and young professionals who are re-examining everything — and tell them no answers are required to belong.
allocations.primary_persona: Jordan, the Exile Coming Home Slowly

section_intents.section_job: Making sense of faith in the modern world — for the young professionals and students of Paradox.
Deconstruction is normal here: walk through what still feels true, what no longer fits — with people doing the same.
The rhythm: midweek discussion group Wednesdays at 7, two to four times a month, plus socials.
Come to one midweek gathering — curiosity is the only prerequisite.
Close alongside: your spiritual journey is worthy of exploration.

allocations.page_job: Prove the vision is actual: Paradox exists for Redlands, with named partners, annual rhythms, and a way in.
allocations.primary_persona: Jordan, the Exile Coming Home Slowly

section_intents.section_job: Actions speak louder than words — make a tangible difference in the here and now.
Why we serve: an outward posture, because the picture of success is a more loving city.

sources.note: Sub-brand flagged 'needs development' in brand handoff — copy should not over-promise a developed program.

section_intents.section_job: The partners, by name: Redlands Family Services, YouthHope, Time for Change Foundation, Ronald McDonald House, and the Good Friday ecumenical circle.

sources.note: 5 partners exceed content_featured_a max_items 3 — outline-page should split across two rows or use career_section(3)+pair; flagged for bind.

section_intents.section_job: The annual rhythm anyone can join: Hunger Walk in June, Thanksgiving Food Drive in November, Christmas Shoe Drive in December.
Join the next outreach event — we would love to have you with us.
Close quietly: some of that started here.

allocations.page_job: Help regulars find their team: eight named teams with real roles, one form, zero guilt.

section_intents.section_job: Your presence and participation make Paradox work — on stage or behind the scenes.
New or long-time, your gifts — whatever they are — have a place here.
The eight teams, with the actual roles on each: Hospitality, Service, Tech, Discussion Groups, Social Planning, Outreach Planning, Special Services, Prayer & Care.

sources.note: 8 teams — cms_managed 'volunteers' type per content collection; exceeds static slot counts by design.

section_intents.section_job: Pick a team and raise your hand — one form.
Close with gratitude: volunteering here is how the welcome gets made.

allocations.page_job: Give the post-visit reader a pathway sized to their readiness: coffee, membership, baptism — present for the ready, never pushed on the unready.

section_intents.section_job: Participate at any level that feels aligned for you right now.
The pathway at a glance: coffee with the pastor → membership → baptism — steps, not a funnel.
Step one, human-sized: coffee with Pastor Craig, in person or on Zoom.
For the ready: membership (vote, serve on boards) and baptism — with real forms and zero countdown clocks.
The recommended first move: get that coffee on the calendar.
Close alongside: whatever level feels aligned, the door is already open.

allocations.page_job: Surface the weekly rhythm and the seasonal moments — WordPress-managed so the team can keep it alive, with Christmas theming ready.

section_intents.section_job: Here's what's happening at Paradox — and you're invited to all of it.
Upcoming events, rendered live from WordPress — outreach days, socials, special services.

sources.note: cms_managed: events type, wordpress_managed per Stage 1 tech flags; supports Christmas seasonal theming goal.

section_intents.section_job: The annual rhythm worth planning around: Easter Sunrise at Appleton Ranch, Women's Empowerment Service, Kids Takeover, Mom's/Dad's Day Out, Fall Social, Coffee & Canvas — and the 10-Year Celebration.

sources.note: Sub-brand flagged 'needs development' — name it lightly, don't build a program page around it.

section_intents.section_job: Pick one and come — most events need nothing from you but showing up.
Close with the weekly constant: Saturdays at 10, kids at 9:15, 51 W Olive.

allocations.page_job: Make reaching a real person effortless: address, parking, hours, forms, socials — with NAP consistency for local SEO.

section_intents.section_job: We'd love to connect — real people answer these.
Where and when: 51 W Olive (chapel on the courtyard), parking off Cajon & Olive, office hours Tue-Fri 9-4, mail to PO Box 8093.
The forms that reach a human: say hello, request prayer, grab coffee with the pastor.
Stay connected between Saturdays: weekly newsletter, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook.
Send the note — someone will actually reply.
Close with the standing welcome: Saturdays at 10, and our inbox in between.

allocations.page_job: A low-pressure giving page that honors 'we only ask once a week': gratitude-forward, one general fund, effortless methods.

section_intents.section_job: Share the gift of paradox — your donations make Paradox possible.
No pressure, by policy: money is asked for once a week in the service, guests aren't expected to give, and one general fund keeps ministries in harmony.
Ways to give, all effortless: online via Church Center, Venmo, or in person on Saturday — with annual statements each January.
Give once or set it and forget it — either way, thank you.
Close on gratitude, not need: thanks to your generosity, this community stays open to everyone.

Stage 1 explicitly parked this topic for build-time audit.
Craig's personal cell from the AM handoff; publishing requires explicit partner consent — main line (909) 255-6065 is placed instead.
